escalation.rationale: US-Iran war enters third week with Iran escalating strikes on Gulf bases and Amazon infrastructure in Bahrain, Houthis declaring a naval blockade of Saudi ports threatening 7% of global oil supply, 18 US troops killed, a 10-day ceasefire proposal from mediators under active review, and Brent crude topping $91/barrel amid Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks.

ticker_items: Iran strikes US radar and air-defense sites in Gulf; warns of 'more decisive' attacks.
Houthis declare naval blockade of Saudi oil ports, threatening 7% of global supply.
Mediators present 10-day US-Iran ceasefire proposal; Washington reviewing while planning offensive.
Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's commander-in-chief amid days of public protests.
Brent crude tops $91/barrel; US gas prices exceed $4 as Hormuz tanker attacked.

eastern_europe.description: Ukraine launched approximately 400 drones overnight targeting the Moscow region, wounding two people and setting several facilities ablaze.

middle_east.description: Iran targeted US radar and air-defense installations across Gulf states with missiles and drones, warning of more powerful attacks to come.
Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed they struck Amazon data hub infrastructure in Bahrain as part of declared all-out war with the United States.
US completed its latest round of airstrikes on Iranian military installations in the ongoing 2026 conflict, marking the tenth consecutive night of strikes.
Houthi rebels declared a naval blockade on Saudi Arabian oil shipments through the Bab al-Mandeb Strait, raising Red Sea war-risk insurance costs.
Another tanker was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz as renewed hostilities continue to empty the waterway of commercial shipping.
Sirens sounded in Bahrain and Jordan as both countries announced interception of two waves of Iranian missiles and drones.

today.incident: US troops killed in Iranian strikes over two weeks
US-Israel attacks on Iran cumulative dead (Al Jazeera tracker)
Ukraine drone strike on Moscow region

claimed_totals.claim: Iran and Lebanon dead in US-Israel attacks
US troops killed in Iranian strikes
US troops wounded in Iranian strikes

russia.text: The deployment of EU and NATO military contingents to Ukraine is absolutely unacceptable for Russia and Moscow cannot allow this.
russia.speaker: Dmitry Peskov
russia.title: Kremlin Spokesman

ukraine.text: Zelenskyy pushed out Ukraine's top general nearly a week after sacking the defense minister.
ukraine.speaker: Volodymyr Zelenskyy
ukraine.title: President of Ukraine

middle_east.text: Iran's conflict with the United States is now a full-scale war.
middle_east.speaker: Iranian Government
middle_east.title: Official Statement

middle_east.text: Iran will deliver more decisive and powerful attacks against US targets.
middle_east.speaker: Iranian Military
middle_east.title: Official Warning

observers.description: Qatar is co-leading mediation efforts alongside Egypt and Pakistan, presenting a 10-day ceasefire proposal to both the US and Iran. Qatar's diplomatic role remains central to de-escalation efforts in the Gulf conflict.

North Korea reaffirmed full support for Russia's war on Ukraine as foreign ministers met in Moscow. Speculation is rising that Kim Jong Un may visit Russia soon, potentially coordinating expanded military cooperation.

Turkey is reportedly poised to open a new containment front against Russia in the Black Sea by committing to Ukraine's maritime security. This represents a significant strategic shift that analysts say poses a serious threat to Russian naval operations.

China tensions dominated the ASEAN ministerial agenda alongside the Iran war, with Southeast Asian governments weighing strategic partnerships. War on the Rocks published analysis on China exploring cognitive warfare concepts targeting Taiwan.

Houthi rebels announced a naval blockade of Saudi oil exports through the Bab al-Mandeb Strait, threatening roughly 7% of global oil supply. The Houthis also declared they would blockade Saudi ports and attacked Gulf shipping in solidarity with Iran.

Hezbollah faces an uncertain future as Lebanese public support wanes amid the broader US-Iran conflict. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun is set to meet Trump to discuss diplomacy with Israel and Washington's support for Lebanon's stabilization agenda.

Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babis dismissed Western-backed pro-Ukraine military coalitions as 'all talk,' signaling growing skepticism within NATO's eastern flank toward coordinated military assistance frameworks for Ukraine.

financial.notes: Brent crude briefly topped $91/barrel as the US-Iran conflict threatens Strait of Hormuz shipping and the Houthi blockade of Bab al-Mandeb puts approximately 6 million barrels per day of Asian-bound crude at risk, pushing US retail gas prices above $4/gallon.

update_log.text: Al Jazeera reports Zelenskyy has replaced Ukraine's commander-in-chief after days of public protests following the defense minister's sacking.
War on the Rocks publishes analysis on how the US-Iran war is reshaping key US bilateral relationships beyond the Middle East.
Red Sea war-risk insurance costs rise after Houthis formally declare naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, per insurance industry sources.
Rigzone confirms another tanker attacked in the Strait of Hormuz as Houthi blockade threat deepens regional maritime risk.
Iran's IRGC claims strike on Amazon data hub infrastructure in Bahrain; US completes tenth consecutive night of airstrikes on Iranian military sites.
Qatar, Egypt, and Pakistan present a 10-day US-Iran ceasefire proposal; Washington reviewing while continuing military planning, per Axios and Hurriyet Daily News.
Iran steps up missile and drone strikes on US radar and air-defense sites in the Gulf; Bahrain and Jordan intercept two waves of attacks.
Brent crude tops $91/barrel and US retail gas prices exceed $4/gallon as Hormuz and Bab al-Mandeb shipping disruptions intensify.
Leaked US intel report reveals 18 American troops killed and approximately 100 wounded in Iranian strikes over the past two weeks.
North Korea reaffirms full support for Russia in Ukraine war as foreign ministers meet in Moscow; Kim Jong Un visit to Russia speculated.
Kremlin warns any EU or NATO troop deployment to Ukraine is absolutely unacceptable and cannot be allowed.
Ukraine launched approximately 400 drones at the Moscow region overnight, wounding two and damaging several facilities.
Secretary of State Rubio arrives in Manila for ASEAN ministerial, overshadowed by Iran war energy fears and China tensions.
